mechanism_narrative: Sustained increases in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) reflect broad economic strength and corporate hiring, leading to higher expected earnings and positive equity returns over the medium term as the economy expands.
failure_condition: The signal's walk-forward information coefficient (IC) is consistently negative or statistically indistinguishable from zero over multiple 3-year rolling periods.

mechanism_narrative: When the effective federal funds rate exceeds a high threshold, it indicates restrictive monetary policy that increases the discount rate for future corporate earnings and tightens financial conditions, leading to negative equity returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 20-day returns are positive in more than 55% of instances when FEDFUNDS is above 4.5% during a tightening cycle.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneously slowing industrial production and accelerating inflation create a stagflationary environment. This combination erodes corporate profit margins and increases economic uncertainty, leading to negative equity market performance.
failure_condition: A regression of 60-day forward returns on interaction terms of INDPRO and CPIAUCSL changes fails to produce a statistically significant negative coefficient (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: In a monetary tightening regime, a low unemployment rate signals an overheating labor market, increasing wage-price spiral risks and forcing the central bank to maintain a restrictive policy stance, which negatively impacts equity valuations.
failure_condition: When FEDFUNDS 3-month change is positive, instances of UNRATE < 5.0% are followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns more than 50% of the time.

mechanism_narrative: A significant month-over-month decline in housing starts (HOUST) indicates weakening consumer demand and construction activity, which are leading indicators of a broader economic slowdown, thus predicting negative short-term equity returns.
failure_condition: The strategy of shorting the S&P 500 after a >5% monthly drop in HOUST fails to generate a positive Sharpe ratio over a full business cycle.

mechanism_narrative: Positive momentum in industrial production (INDPRO) reflects a robust manufacturing and supply side of the economy, translating into higher corporate revenues and positive equity market sentiment over very short horizons.
failure_condition: A simple momentum signal based on INDPRO (e.g., 3-month change) shows a near-zero or negative rank correlation with 5-day forward S&P 500 returns over a 10-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp, accelerating increase in the Consumer Price Index (CPIAUCSL) signals that inflation is not under control, increasing the probability of more aggressive monetary tightening by the central bank, which is negative for equities.
failure_condition: Periods where YoY CPI is above 5% are not followed by statistically significant negative average 20-day returns compared to periods where it is below 5% (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in real retail sales indicates weakening consumer spending power. This directly implies lower future corporate revenues and earnings, leading to negative equity returns over the next quarter.
candidate_trigger: Year-over-year % change in RSAFS < Year-over-year % change in CPIAUCSL
failure_condition: A long-short portfolio based on the sign of real retail sales growth (nominal sales growth minus inflation) fails to achieve a positive information ratio over a 10-year period.
